complexityAnalysis.task_title: Anchor Program Core Account Structures & Foundation Instructions
complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 6 subtasks covering: (1) Anchor workspace initialization and Cargo.toml/Anchor.toml configuration; (2) PDA account structures in state/ modules — OperatorConfig, CustomerBalance, AgentPackage, TaskReceipt with correct seeds, fields, and space calculations; (3) utils/ module with SHA-256 hash_seed helper and program vault PDA definition; (4) Initialization and account creation instructions — initialize_operator, create_customer_account, register_agent_package, update_agent_package; (5) Token flow instructions — deposit, withdraw with vault PDA signing, plus update_spending_caps, pause/unpause; (6) Comprehensive error enum and smoke test suite (6 tests per test_strategy). Subtasks 2-3 can be parallel. Subtasks 4-5 depend on 2-3. Subtask 6 depends on all. All subtasks need an implementer subagent; subtask 6 also needs a tester subagent.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is the foundational Anchor program with 4 PDA account structures, 9 instructions spanning token transfers and state management, a utility module, error enum, and vault PDA. It involves multiple Rust source files across state/, instructions/, and utils/ modules, complex Anchor constraints, and SPL token integration. The IDL output is a critical dependency for 5 downstream tasks.

complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 5 subtasks: (1) settle_task instruction scaffolding — account struct definition with optional agent_package/author_ata accounts, validation chain (paused, auth, per-task cap, daily cap with day rollover, balance check); (2) Settlement Case 1 (no agent package) — full debit and transfer to treasury with PDA signing; (3) Settlement Cases 2 and 3 — quality_met=true split logic (author_amount calculation, dual transfers, AgentPackage counter updates) and quality_met=false zero-charge path; (4) refund_task instruction — status validation, treasury-to-vault transfer, customer balance restoration, status update to Refunded with hackathon simplification comment; (5) Anchor events (TaskSettled, TaskRefunded, Deposited, Withdrawn, AgentPackageRegistered) and 8-case test suite. Consider whether to use Optional accounts or two instruction variants for the agent_package. Subtasks 1 is a prerequisite for 2-3. Subtask 4 can parallel 2-3. Subtask 5 depends on all.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: The settlement engine contains the core business logic with a 3-case branching flow, daily cap rollover computation, optional accounts pattern, dual token transfers with PDA signing, and counter updates across multiple accounts. The refund instruction adds treasury-to-vault reverse flow. Eight targeted test cases covering all paths add verification complexity.

complexityAnalysis.task_title: Comprehensive Anchor Program Integration Test Suite
complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 5 subtasks: (1) Test infrastructure setup — helper functions (createMockMint, createAta, mintTo, all PDA derivation helpers with SHA-256), keypair generation, Mocha configuration, test:ci script in package.json; (2) Groups 1-3: Initialization, Customer Account Management, Agent Package Registration tests including error cases (re-init, duplicate PDA, non-author update, invalid split_bps); (3) Groups 4-5: Deposits & Withdrawals and Settlement Case 1 (no agent package) — verify balances, totals, TaskReceipt fields; (4) Groups 6-8: Settlement Cases 2 & 3 (quality split and quality-not-met) and Spending Cap Enforcement tests including daily cap rollover; (5) Groups 9-12: Circuit Breaker (pause/unpause), Refund Flow, Idempotency (duplicate task_id), and Authorization tests. Subtask 1 is prerequisite for all others. Subtasks 2-5 can be developed in parallel. All need tester subagents. Critical: PDA derivation must use SHA-256 matching on-chain logic.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: 30+ test cases across 12 test groups covering all 11 instructions, 3 settlement cases, all error codes, and lifecycle flows. Requires complex test infrastructure with mock mint setup, ATA creation, PDA derivation matching on-chain SHA-256 logic, and careful state management across sequential test phases.

complexityAnalysis.task_title: Controller Settlement Event Publisher & Receipt Storage
complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 5 subtasks: (1) Feature flag setup — add solana-billing feature to Cargo.toml with optional dependencies (redis, serde_json, sha2), SolanaBillingConfig struct, and customer_solana_pubkey field; (2) Event and receipt schema definitions — SettlementEvent and ReceiptJson structs with serialization in billing/ module; (3) Receipt storage — S3 upload logic, SHA-256 hash computation, receipt JSON generation; (4) Redis stream publisher — async connection, XADD to cto:settlements stream, non-blocking fire-and-forget error handling; (5) Controller reconciliation loop integration — billing amount computation from pod duration/tier, quality determination from attestations, event publishing, CodeRun annotation, all behind #[cfg(feature = "solana-billing")]. Subtasks 1-2 are prerequisites. Subtasks 3-4 can be parallel. Subtask 5 depends on all. Verify feature-gated compilation both with and without the flag.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Cross-cutting concern touching multiple integration points: Redis stream publishing, S3 receipt storage, SHA-256 hashing, controller reconciliation loop modification, and feature-gated compilation. Requires careful non-blocking error handling to ensure the controller is never blocked by billing failures. New billing/ module with multiple sub-modules.

complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 6 subtasks: (1) Crate scaffolding and configuration — new crate at crates/settlement-sidecar/, SidecarConfig with environment variable loading, Cargo.toml with solana-sdk/solana-client/redis/tokio dependencies; (2) Redis stream consumer — consumer group creation, XREADGROUP main loop with BLOCK, pending message recovery on startup, XACK on success, DLQ routing on max retries; (3) Solana transaction builder — PDA derivation with SHA-256 matching on-chain logic, settle_task and refund_task instruction construction with all required accounts, optional agent_package/author_ata handling; (4) Submission and retry logic — send_and_confirm with blockhash caching/refresh, exponential backoff, error classification (retryable vs. program-level), DLQ writing; (5) Health endpoint and observability — HTTP /healthz at :8080, structured JSON logging via tracing-subscriber, metrics counters; (6) Dockerfile and integration test — multi-stage Docker build, integration test with local validator + Redis verifying end-to-end event→transaction→on-chain receipt flow. Subtasks 1 is prerequisite. Subtasks 2-4 form the core pipeline (2→3→4 sequential dependency). Subtask 5 can parallel 3-4. Subtask 6 depends on all.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Entirely new standalone Rust binary with three external system integrations (Redis, Solana RPC, health HTTP). Complex consumer group pattern with pending message recovery, dead-letter queue routing, exponential backoff retry logic, and blockhash management. PDA derivation must exactly match on-chain logic. Integration testing requires orchestrating local validator and Redis.

complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 4 subtasks: (1) Project setup and utilities — TypeScript project at demo/cli/, PDA derivation helpers with SHA-256, mock USDC mint/ATA creation helpers, Explorer link generator, chalk/ora formatting utilities, CLI argument parsing (--cluster, --keypair); (2) Phases 0-3 — Setup & Token Provisioning (keypair generation, SOL airdrop, mint creation, ATA creation, USDC minting), Initialize Operator, Register Agent Package, Create Customer Account; (3) Phases 4-7 — Customer Deposits, three settlement scenarios (quality met with split, quality not met, no agent package) with colored output highlighting splits; (4) Phases 8-9 — On-chain receipt verification with formatted table display, balance withdrawal with final summary, error handling for all phases, npm scripts (demo:localnet, demo:devnet). Subtask 1 is prerequisite. Subtasks 2-3 are sequential (state depends on prior phases). Subtask 4 depends on 3.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: 10 sequential demo phases covering the full billing and marketplace flow. Requires TypeScript PDA derivation matching on-chain SHA-256 logic, SPL token operations (mint creation, ATA management), all program instruction calls, formatted console output with colors/spinners, and Explorer link generation. Moderate complexity but single-domain and well-defined.

complexityAnalysis.task_title: Hackathon Demo Web Dashboard - Core Setup & Wallet Integration
complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 4 subtasks: (1) Next.js 14 project initialization — create-next-app at demo/web/, install all dependencies (@coral-xyz/anchor, @solana/web3.js, wallet adapter packages), shadcn/ui init with dark theme and component installation (button, card, badge, table, dialog, input, tabs, separator, toast); (2) Tailwind theme and global styles — custom Solana colors (purple #9945FF, green #14F195, dark #0E0E1A, card #1A1A2E), CSS variable mapping for shadcn, dark mode class strategy, Inter font; (3) Wallet integration and layout — WalletProvider/ConnectionProvider/WalletModalProvider in app layout, Header component with logo/network badge/WalletMultiButton, split-panel layout (60/40 desktop, stacked mobile), 'Not Connected' state; (4) Shared hooks and types — useProgram(), usePdaDerivation() with SHA-256, useCustomerBalance(), useOperatorConfig(), TypeScript type mirrors, formatUsdc/formatQuality utilities. Subtasks 1-2 can be parallel. Subtask 3 depends on 1-2. Subtask 4 depends on 1.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Standard Next.js project setup but with Solana wallet adapter integration adding complexity. Custom theme with Solana branding, shadcn/ui component library initialization, split-panel responsive layout, and four custom hooks that interface with the Anchor program. The hooks require PDA derivation matching on-chain SHA-256 logic.

complexityAnalysis.task_title: Hackathon Demo Web Dashboard - Settlement Feed & Balance Management
complexityAnalysis.expansion_prompt: Break this task into 5 subtasks: (1) Settlement Feed DataTable component — shadcn DataTable with columns for Task ID, Amount, Author Earned, Quality (color-coded badges: green for met, red for not met), Status, timestamp; fetch TaskReceipt PDAs and display in real-time or polling; (2) Customer Balance Card — display current balance, total deposited, total spent, task count, spending caps (max_per_task, max_per_day, daily_spent); visual balance bar or progress indicator; (3) Deposit/Withdraw interactive flows — dialog/modal forms for deposit amount and withdraw amount, transaction submission via Anchor program, toast notifications for success/failure, balance refresh after transaction; (4) Agent Package listing and details — fetch and display registered AgentPackages with author, split_bps, total_earned, success_rate (success_count/task_count), active status badge; (5) Receipt verification view — click a TaskReceipt row to see full details including receipt_hash, on-chain Explorer link, quality_met status with explanation. All components must use the hooks from Task 7 and handle wallet disconnected state gracefully. Optimize visual layout for 1920×1080 demo recording.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Multiple interactive React components with on-chain data fetching, transaction submission flows, real-time or polling data refresh, and careful UI/UX for demo purposes. The settlement feed requires parsing TaskReceipt PDAs, deposit/withdraw needs transaction building and confirmation, and the agent package listing needs computed metrics. Color-coded quality indicators and responsive layout add to complexity.
